newsSinceJun6: Digitimes (Jun 8) reported Macronix posted a single-month record May 2026 revenue of ~NT$6.26B, with NOR flash up ~47% YoY on AI server/HPC demand; management has shifted to monthly pricing with continued hikes as Samsung's SLC NAND exit leaves a supply vacuum. Meanwhile the stock has pulled back roughly 20% from its June 2 all-time high (~NT$180) to NT$145.5, so the +1.4% day move is a modest rebound, not a chase.

sources.label: Digitimes - Adata, Macronix report record May revenue amid rising memory demand, prices (Jun 8, 2026)
BigGo Finance - Macronix FY2026 Q1 earnings call (EPS NT$0.9, GM 40.8%, 'each quarter better than the last')
Smartkarma - Macronix: NOR flash shortage intensifies as AI racks drive new type of demand

verdicts.rankRationale: New hard data since yesterday's hold: record May revenue (NT$6.26B, NOR +47% YoY on AI servers) confirms the memory-tightness thesis, while a ~20% pullback from the June 2 all-time high restores some asymmetry at rank 98 - the sell-off plus thesis-confirming evidence warrants a modest move up, not a chase.
verdicts.bull: Monthly revenue records (April, then May), monthly repricing with continued hikes, and Samsung's SLC NAND exit give Macronix structural pricing power in NOR/SLC niches tied to every AI rack; Q1 GM already rebounded to 40.8% with management guiding each quarter better.
verdicts.neutral: The thesis is now well-covered (Digitimes, Smartkarma, sell-side notes) and +235% YTD means much of the rerating has happened; record monthly revenue confirms rather than extends the story, so this is incremental, not thesis-changing.
verdicts.bear: Specialty memory cycles turn fast: NT$22B capex restart plus industry supply responses could normalize NOR/SLC pricing into 2027, and a stock that fell ~20% off its high in a week shows how flow-sensitive the register has become.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: July 28 Q2 earnings confirming sequential margin/ASP gains; further NOR/SLC price-hike announcements.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Memory pricing rolls over or AI server NOR content gets second-sourced, deflating a +235% YTD rerating built on a tight-supply narrative.
